text: Genotype probabilities get stored fst databases chromosome rapid access. takes steps, starting creating directory store files calculating genotype probabilities allele pairs.
code: # Create directory for fst data; must call it "genoprob" fst_dir <- file.path(project_dir, "genoprob") dir.create(fst_dir)  # Calculate allele pair genotype probabilities probs <- qtl2::calc_genoprob(recla, gmap, err=0.002) # Convert allele pair probs to fst via qtl2fst fprobs <- qtl2pattern::fst_genoprob(probs, "probs", fst_dir) saveRDS(fprobs, file = file.path(fst_dir, "fprobs.rds"))  # Compute allele genotype probabilities from those for allele pairs aprobs <- genoprob_to_alleleprob(probs) # Convert allele probs to fst via qtl2fst faprobs <- fst_genoprob(aprobs, "aprobs", fst_dir) saveRDS(faprobs, file = file.path(fst_dir, "faprobs.rds"))

title: Module Architecture
text: Purpose: Allows users load experimental projects, select phenotype datasets, run genome-wide hotspot scans, view specific raw phenotype distributions. Entrypoint: R/hotspotApp.R R/hotspotDataApp.R: Computes hotspot object calculating density precomputed QTL peaks across chromosomes. R/hotspotPlotApp.R: Generates plots hotspot scans (LOD peak count vs. genomic coordinates). R/hotspotTableApp.R: Renders searchable data table peaks within selected hotspot. R/peakApp.R: Manages peak summaries filtered hotspot. R/peakReadApp.R: Performs file reading peaks.rds data file. R/phenoApp.R: Acts container module phenotype distribution panels. R/phenoReadApp.R: Reads pheno_data.rds matrix. R/phenoNamesApp.R: Controls selectors phenotype name filtering. R/phenoDataApp.R: Normalizes filters phenotype values (e.g. rank-Z transformation via rankZ()). R/phenoTableApp.R: Displays raw normalized phenotype values. R/phenoPlotApp.R: Plots boxplots, scatterplots, density distributions.

title: Module Architecture
text: Purpose: Computes compares genome-wide scans using multi-parent allele founder coefficients versus high-density SNP association mapping. Entrypoint: R/scanApp.R R/scanDataApp.R: Computes plots allele founder coefficients scans (LOD/BLUP curves) selected chromosomes. R/snpGeneApp.R: Top-level wrapper linking SNP tables, SNP plots, gene region overlays, exon plots. R/snpTableApp.R: Lists top SNPs/variants region. R/snpPlotApp.R: Renders Manhattan-style SNP association LOD scan. R/geneRegionApp.R: Queries gene annotation databases draws gene models window. R/geneExonApp.R: Pulls detailed exon structures clicked queried gene.

title: Module Architecture
text: Purpose: Groups high-density SNPs QTL region Strain Distribution Patterns (SDPs) narrow candidates based shared founder ancestral alleles. Entrypoint: R/patternApp.R R/snpPatternApp.R: Top-level module coordinating pattern scans, features, plotting. R/patternDataApp.R: Runs underlying scan top SDPs generates summaries. R/patternPlotApp.R: Generates SDP scan plots comparing multiple phenotypes. R/snpFeatureApp.R: Maps variants genomic consequences (synonymous, coding, intron, splice site, etc.) catalogs SDP details.

title: Module Architecture
text: Purpose: Inspects multi-point raw founder genotype probabilities, strain distribution pattern assignments, phenotypic effects specific genomic locations. Entrypoint: R/genoApp.R R/genoDataApp.R: Renders genotype probabilities SDP pairings chosen physical marker. deep-dive, see Genotypes Panel Guide. R/genoPlotApp.R: Visualizes individuals’ genotype probabilities along chromosomal segments. R/genoEffectApp.R: Evaluates phenotypic averages genotype group, providing tables plots genetic effects.

title: Module Architecture
text: Purpose: Displays scatter plots selected phenotypes, grouped colored ancestral strain distribution patterns (SDPs), sex, diet. Entrypoint: R/scatterApp.R R/scatterPlotApp.R: Reusable generic plotting module managing aesthetic mappings, open symbol selections, linetypes.

title: Module Architecture
text: Purpose: Performs regression-based QTL mediation analysis (e.g., intermediate mRNA expression protein abundance) identify candidate causal drivers. Entrypoint: R/mediateApp.R R/mediateDataApp.R: Calls mediation libraries (qtl2mediate) compute drop QTL LOD score conditioning mediators. R/mediatePlotApp.R: Visualizes mediation results (LOD drops vs. physical coordinates). R/triadApp.R: Plots individual scatterplot grids illustrating relationships among driver locus, mediator, target phenotype.

title: Module Architecture
text: modules represent individual analysis panels act shared global selectors data managers. essential feeding reactive parameters multiple tabs: R/projectApp.R: Loads initial project list projects.csv allows user switch taxa, databases, study setups. R/setParApp.R: Dynamically extracts study-specific parameters (phenotype class subject_model) depending selected project registry. R/winParApp.R: Tracks active chromosomal window (Chr, Start/End Mbp) selected peak hotspot table. R/dipParApp.R: Manages genetic model parameters (additive vs. dominance actions, founder allele codes). R/snpListApp.R: Consolidated input selector SNP filters. handles local coordinates, min LOD score sliders, active phenotype selections. R/probsApp.R: Reactive server utility load high-dimensional multi-point founder genotype probabilities using fast disk-backed serialization (FST database queries). R/kinshipApp.R: Reactively loads LOCO (Leave-One-Chromosome-) kinship matrix objects corresponding active chromosome. R/downloadApp.R: Wrapper server interfacing downr external package manage CSV exports vector plot rendering (PNG/PDF). R/scatterPlotApp.R: Generic reusable module plot static interactive scatter plots (X vs Y, option map Sex, Diet, Genotype Color/Shape/Facet aesthetics).

Configures stroke = 1.5 point geometries, widening circle symbol outlines remain highly legible combined transparency (alpha). Automatically draws solid regression lines (linetype = "solid") underneath scatter symbols, preventing line overlays obscuring point centers. Since strain distribution pattern names can contain colons ":" (e.g., ":B"), faceting avoids formula parsing syntax (interprets colons interaction variables). instead uses tidyverse data pronoun reference: facet_wrap(ggplot2::vars(.data[[facet_var]]))

title: Prepare qtl2 data for qtl2shiny
text: query genes variants usually common across projects taxa. use query routines R/qtl2 CC mouse. create already exist. Note taxa_path relative address. Use create_probs_query_func package ‘qtl2pattern’ query genotype probabilities. Save RDS format later use. Note requires relative address project data provided project_path. data include mRNA data, set null routine. Make sure required arguments.
code: if(file.exists(qname <- file.path(taxa_dir, "query_genes.rds"))) {   warning(paste("gene query", qname,                 "already exists and will not be overwritten")) } else {   query_genes <-      qtl2::create_gene_query_func(       file.path(taxa_path, "mouse_genes.sqlite"))   saveRDS(query_genes, qname) } if(file.exists(qname <- file.path(taxa_dir, "query_variants.rds"))) {   warning(paste("variant query", qname,                 "already exists and will not be overwritten")) } else {   query_variants <-      qtl2::create_variant_query_func(       file.path(taxa_path, "cc_variants.sqlite"))   saveRDS(query_variants, qname) } query_probs <- qtl2pattern::create_probs_query_func(project_path) saveRDS(query_probs, file.path(project_dir, "query_probs.rds")) query_mrna <- qtl2shiny::create_mrna_query_func(NULL) saveRDS(query_mrna, file.path(project_dir, "query_mrna.rds")) if(httr::HEAD(project_data)$status != 200) {   warning(paste("project data for", project_info$project, "does not exist"))   knitr::knit_exit() } qtl2data <- qtl2::read_cross2(project_data) covar <- dplyr::rename(qtl2data$covar, sex = "Sex") saveRDS(covar, file.path(project_dir, "covar.rds")) saveRDS(qtl2data$pheno, file.path(project_dir, "pheno_data.rds")) gmap <- qtl2::insert_pseudomarkers(qtl2data$gmap, step=1) pmap <- qtl2::interp_map(gmap, qtl2data$gmap, qtl2data$pmap) saveRDS(pmap, file.path(project_dir, "pmap.rds"))

title: Prepare qtl2 data for qtl2shiny
text: Genotype probabilities can large best put fast database (“fst” “feather”). Current recommendation “fst”. step takes time, primarily calculating genotype probabilities.
code:
fast <- "fst" if(!dir.exists(genoprob_dir <- file.path(project_dir, "genoprob"))) {   dir.create(genoprob_dir) } faprobs_file <- file.path(genoprob_dir, paste0("aprobs_fstindex.rds")) if(!file.exists(fprobs_file <- file.path(genoprob_dir,                                          paste0("probs_fstindex.rds")))) {   cat("allele pair genotype probabilities ...
", file = stderr())   pr <- qtl2::calc_genoprob(qtl2data, gmap, err=0.002)   cat(paste0("allele pair prob conversion via qtl2", fast, "
"), file = stderr())   fast_genoprob <- switch(fast,                           fst = qtl2fst::fst_genoprob,                           feather = qtl2feather::feather_genoprob)   fprobs <- fast_genoprob(pr, "probs", genoprob_dir, quiet = TRUE)   saveRDS(fprobs, file = fprobs_file)    cat("allele genotype probabilities
", file = stderr())   apr <- qtl2::genoprob_to_alleleprob(pr)   cat(paste0("allele prob conversion via qtl2", fast, "
"), file = stderr())   faprobs <- fast_genoprob(apr, "aprobs", genoprob_dir, quiet = TRUE)   saveRDS(faprobs, file = faprobs_file) } else {   fprobs <- readRDS(fprobs_file)   faprobs <- readRDS(faprobs_file) } kinship_loco <- qtl2::calc_kinship(faprobs, "loco") saveRDS(kinship_loco, file.path(project_dir, "kinship.rds"))

code: form <- formula(paste("~", paste(names(covar), collapse = "+"))) addcovar <- stats::model.matrix(form, covar)[, -1, drop = FALSE] out <- qtl2::scan1(faprobs, qtl2data$pheno, kinship_loco, addcovar=addcovar) peaks <- qtl2::find_peaks(out, pmap, threshold = 3)

code: peaks <-    dplyr::select(     dplyr::mutate(       dplyr::rename(         peaks,         pheno = "lodcolumn"),     longname = .data$pheno,     output = .data$pheno,     pheno_group = "group", # used to group phenotypes     pheno_type = "type"),  # used to type phenotypes   -.data$lodindex) analyses_tbl <-    dplyr::mutate(     dplyr::select(       dplyr::distinct(         peaks,         .data$pheno,         .keep_all = TRUE),     -.data$lod, -.data$pos, -.data$chr),   model = "normal",   transf = "identity",   offset = 0,   winsorize = FALSE) for(i in names(covar))   analyses_tbl[[i]] <- TRUE saveRDS(peaks, file.path(project_dir, "peaks.rds")) saveRDS(analyses_tbl, file.path(project_dir, "analyses.rds"))
